light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: Toccoa River fishing outlook is fair to good with recent stocking enhancing trout activity. Expect fair conditions with balanced nymph approaches working best.
generated_text.best_window: Day 1 morning is the best window because trout position in runs, seams, and structure under neutral light and pressure, favoring a balanced nymph approach supported by recent stocking.
generated_text.current_conditions: USGS reports flow at 233 cfs, gage height 1.22 ft, water temperature and turbidity data are unavailable.
generated_text.weather_setup: Current conditions show 32.62% average sky cover, pressure at 30.04 inHg with no clear trend, light wind averaging 2.4 mph, and a small chance of rain around 44% with negligible expected inches.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will favor feeding lanes close to cover, responding best to clean drifts and smaller natural patterns. Use egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell attractors due to recent stocking; focus on runs, seams, and structure with a balanced nymph approach.
